tool_args.label: Bx41 Select Bus Service on Webster Avenue

payload.description: Bx41 Select Bus Service along Webster Avenue launched June 30, 2013, with off-board fare collection, offset bus lanes, intersection traffic improvements, and enhanced stations.
payload.project_name: Bx41 Select Bus Service on Webster Avenue

evidence_refs.source_quote: On June 30, 2013, MTA New York City Transit (NYCT) and New York City Department of Transportation (NYCDOT) launched the Bx41 SBS along Webster Avenue.
the Webster Avenue corridor was selected as the first of the Phase II BRT routes to be implemented.

payload.description: Webster Avenue from E 165 Street to E Gun Hill Road in the Bronx, an important north-south corridor connecting The Hub, Fordham Plaza, and Gun Hill Road Intermodal Terminal.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Eight miles of bus lanes (four miles in each direction) were installed on Webster Avenue from E 165 Street to E Gun Hill Road
Webster Avenue is one of the Bronx's important streets
Webster Avenue is an important north-south street through the center of the Bronx

payload.streets: Melrose Avenue, Webster Avenue, East Gun Hill Road

evidence_refs.source_quote: Bx41 SBS along Webster Avenue
The Bx41 is the primary transit service on the corridor, and runs along Melrose Avenue, Webster Avenue and East Gun Hill Road.
Bx41 SBS: A dark blue line running along Webster Avenue from E 161 St to E 204 St, with a callout box stating 'Bx41 SBS replaces the Bx41 LTD'.
the Bx41 SBS operates from approximately 6am to 9pm, seven days a week, with headways between 8 and 12 minutes.

payload.description: Bx15 Limited replaced the duplicative portion of the Bx55 on 3rd Avenue, renamed to clearly indicate its pairing with the Bx15 Local. Interborough Bronx-to-Manhattan segment served by Bx15 Limited.

payload.description: Limited-stop replacement for the 3rd Avenue elevated subway service discontinued in 1973. Operated partly on Webster Avenue and partly on 3rd Avenue. Duplicative portion on northern Webster Avenue eliminated with Bx41 SBS launch.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Bx55, the limited-stop replacement for the 3rd Avenue elevated subway service that was discontinued in 1973, also operated partly on Webster Avenue and partly on 3rd Avenue.
the duplicative portion of the Bx55 on northern Webster Avenue was eliminated

tool_args.label: Offset bus lanes on Webster Avenue

payload.description: Eight miles of bus lanes (four miles each direction) on Webster Avenue from E 165 Street to E Gun Hill Road. Majority are offset bus lanes (one lane away from curb). Painted terracotta red. Curbside bus lanes at some locations in effect Mon-Fri 7am-7pm.
payload.location_text: Webster Avenue from E 165 Street to E Gun Hill Road

normalized_location.raw_text: Webster Avenue from E 165 Street to E Gun Hill Road

evidence_refs.source_quote: Eight miles of bus lanes (four miles in each direction) were installed on Webster Avenue from E 165 Street to E Gun Hill Road to support the launch of the Bx41 SBS.
The majority of the bus lanes on Webster Avenue are offset bus lanes, meaning they are one lane away from the curb.

tool_args.label: Off-board fare collection for Bx41 SBS

payload.description: Off-board fare payment using MetroCard and coin fare machines at each station. Riders obtain a ticket before boarding. Reduced dwell time by 28%.
payload.location_text: Bx41 SBS stations on Webster Avenue

normalized_location.raw_text: Bx41 SBS stations on Webster Avenue

evidence_refs.source_quote: Under the off-board fare collection system, riders pay their fare before boarding, using MetroCard or coin fare machines located at each station to obtain a ticket.
Overall, dwell time on the Bx41 SBS was reduced by 28% thanks to the new payment system.
off-board fare collection

payload.claim_text: Prior to SBS launch, the Bx41 traveled at an average speed of seven miles per hour. For nearly half of its travel time, the bus was stopped at either bus stops or traffic lights. The typical one-way travel time varied by more than 20 minutes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Prior to the launch of SBS, the Bx41 traveled at an average speed of seven miles per hour.
the typical one-way travel time varied by more than 20 minutes.
